---
title: "Train Travel in Italy"
subtitle: "Trenitalia & Italo Routes"
date: "2026-06-05"
categories: [logistics, trains, transportation]
toc: true
---

## Overview

Italian trains are efficient, comfortable, and one of the best ways to travel between cities. Our trip involves three major train journeys, all bookable through Trenitalia or Italo (Italy's two main rail operators).

**Key Routes:**
1. **Pisa → Florence** (June 5): Regional train, 1 hour
2. **Florence → Naples** (June 10): High-speed Frecciarossa/Italo, 2.5 hours
3. **Naples → Brindisi** (June 13): Scenic journey through Puglia, 5-6 hours

## Booking Websites

- **[Trenitalia.com](https://www.trenitalia.com)** - Italy's national rail operator
- **[Italotreno.it](https://www.italotreno.it)** - Private high-speed operator

**Booking Window:** 2-4 weeks ahead for best "Super Economy" fares

**Payment:** Major credit cards accepted, often cheaper to book directly vs. third-party sites

---

## Route 1: Pisa → Florence (June 5)

### Overview

**Type:** Regional train (Regionale)
**Duration:** 50-70 minutes
**Frequency:** Every 30 minutes, ~52 trains per day
**Price:** €10-€12 one way
**Advance Booking:** Not required (fixed price)

### Step-by-Step from Pisa Airport

1. **PisaMover Shuttle**
   - Automated people mover from airport to Pisa Centrale station
   - Duration: 8 minutes
   - Runs continuously
   - Free or minimal cost

2. **Pisa Centrale to Firenze SMN**
   - Direct regional train (no changes)
   - Departures every 30 minutes
   - No seat reservations (general seating)
   - Luggage racks available on train

3. **Arrive Firenze SMN**
   - Florence's main station (Santa Maria Novella)
   - Located in historic center
   - Easy walk or short taxi to most hotels

### Schedule

| First Train | Last Train |
|-------------|------------|
| 4:15 AM from Pisa Centrale | 9:01 PM (arrives Florence 10:26 PM) |

**Recommended:** Take a mid-morning train after arrival from overnight flight to allow for customs, baggage claim, and coffee.

### Tips

- **No advance booking needed** - just buy ticket at station or online
- Validate ticket before boarding (yellow machines on platform)
- Sit on left side of train for views (though short journey)
- Store luggage in designated areas
- Announcements in Italian and English

---

## Route 2: Florence → Naples (June 10)

### Overview

This is one of Italy's premier high-speed train routes - fast, frequent, and spectacular.

**Type:** Frecciarossa (Trenitalia) or Italo
**Duration:** 2h 29m - 3h 00m
**Frequency:** Every 30-60 minutes
**Stations:** Firenze SMN → Napoli Centrale

### Fare Classes

| Class | Price | Features |
|-------|-------|----------|
| **Super Economy** | €20-€50 | Advance purchase, limited flexibility |
| **Economy** | €35-€65 | Standard flexibility |
| **Business/Prima** | €50-€120 | More space, quieter car, snacks included |

**Recommendation:** Book Economy or Business class 2-4 weeks ahead for best pricing.

### What to Expect

**Frecciarossa Features:**
- Modern, clean trains
- Comfortable reclining seats
- Power outlets at seats
- WiFi (sometimes unreliable)
- Quiet cars available
- Café/bar car
- Luggage storage areas

**Seat Selection:**
- Window seats for views of Italian countryside
- Table seats if traveling with companion
- Solo seats (1+2 configuration in some cars)

### Scenic Highlights

The route passes through:
- Tuscan countryside
- Apennine mountains
- Approaches to Naples with Vesuvius views

**Best Views:** Sit on the right side (west) approaching Naples for potential Vesuvius sighting.

### Stations

**Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN)**
- Central Florence station
- Arrive 15-20 minutes early
- Multiple platforms (check departure boards)
- Luggage storage available

**Napoli Centrale**
- Main Naples station
- Can be chaotic - watch belongings
- Taxi stand outside
- Metro connections
- Hotel pickups coordinate here

---

## Route 3: Naples → Brindisi (Puglia) (June 14)

### Overview

This is a journey through the heart of Southern Italy - one of the most scenic and culturally rich train rides in the country.

**Type:** Intercity (IC) express — direct service, no changes required
**Duration:** 5-5.5 hours
**Frequency:** ~5-6 daily departures
**Price:** €15-€40 First Class, €10-€25 Second Class
**Advance Booking:** 2-4 weeks for best fares

### Route Options

| Route | Train Type | Duration | Changes | Class | Fare |
|-------|------------|----------|---------|-------|------|
| **Naples → Brindisi (IC)** | **Intercity Express** | **~5h 30m** | **0 (Direct)** | **1st + 2nd** | **€15-€40 (1st)** |
| Naples → Bari | Frecciarossa/Italo | 3h 15-4h | 0-1 | Various | €20-€60 |
| Naples → Brindisi (old route) | Frecce + Regional | 5-6h | 1 (Bari) | Various | €22-€75 |
| Naples → Lecce | Frecciarossa | 5h 30-6h | 0-1 | Various | €25-€80 |

**Key Decision:** Intercity (IC) is the clear winner — direct service, First Class available, and no slow regional connections required.

### Is It a Milk Run? First Class Options

**Short answer: Take the Intercity (IC) train — it's NOT a milk run and has First Class seating.**

| Option | Type | Duration | Class | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Intercity (IC)** | Express conventional | ~5h 30m | **1st + 2nd Class** | ✅ Recommended |
| Frecciarossa to Bari + Regional | High-speed + slow regional | 5–6h | Business on FS leg only | Regional leg IS slow (many stops) |

**Why Intercity (IC) is the right choice:**
- Runs the full Naples → Brindisi route **without changing trains**
- Makes major stops only (Foggia, Bari, coastal towns) — not every tiny station
- **First Class** on IC trains: reserved seat, more space, dedicated car, quieter
- IC trains are not Frecciarossa-class but they are proper express trains
- Fares: €15–40 First Class depending on advance booking
- Book at [trenitalia.com](https://www.trenitalia.com) — search Naples Centrale → Brindisi, filter by IC

**What makes the "milk run" concern valid:**
The Bari→Brindisi **regional** (Regionale) connection, if you take the Frecciarossa-then-change-at-Bari approach, IS slow — it stops at many coastal towns. That's the connection to avoid. The IC bypasses this entirely.

**Booking tip:** IC trains must be booked on Trenitalia (Italo doesn't serve this southern route). Book 2–4 weeks ahead for best fares and seat selection.

### Understanding Puglia Geography

**Important:** Brindisi is IN Puglia, not before it. The train journey IS a tour through Puglia:

**Route Path (North to South):**
1. **Caserta** - Royal Palace (Reggia di Caserta), visible from station
2. **Benevento** - Roman Arch of Trajan
3. **Foggia** - **Enter Puglia here** (gateway to Gargano Peninsula)
4. **Bari** - Puglia's capital (~3.5h from Naples)
5. **Polignano a Mare** - Dramatic cliffside town
6. **Ostuni** - "White City" on hilltop
7. **Brindisi** - Historic port city (~5.5h from Naples)
8. **Lecce** - "Florence of the South" (~6h from Naples, 30 min beyond Brindisi)

### Recommended Train

**Intercity (IC) Naples Centrale → Brindisi**
- Morning departure recommended (arrive Brindisi early afternoon)
- Check current schedules at trenitalia.com — schedules change seasonally
- First Class car: look for "Prima Classe" designation when booking
- Journey time: approximately 5h 30min direct

**Departure Times:**
- **Morning Options:** Earliest ~8:12 AM, Latest ~6:12 PM
- **Strategy:** Take morning train to maximize daylight views through Puglia

### Scenic Journey Highlights

**What You'll See:**
- Crossing the Apennines (mountains)
- Entering the Tavoliere delle Puglie (plains)
- Adriatic coastline (sit on LEFT side after Foggia)
- Olive groves stretching to horizon
- Whitewashed towns on hillsides

### Recommended Approach

**Option A: Direct to Brindisi (Simple)**
- Morning Frecce train from Naples
- Arrive Brindisi early afternoon (~5.5 hours)
- Meet tour group or check into hotel
- One journey, minimal hassle

**Option B: Stop in Bari (Recommended if Time Allows)**
- Take morning train to Bari (3.5h)
- Spend afternoon/evening in Bari Vecchia (old town)
- Watch orecchiette ladies making pasta
- Visit Basilica di San Nicola
- Fresh seafood dinner along the port
- **Next morning:** Easy 1-1.5 hour regional train to Brindisi

**Bari Benefits:**
- Breaks up long journey
- Taste of Puglia before tour begins
- Beautiful old town (Bari Vecchia)
- Day trip options: Matera (cave dwellings, 1h), Alberobello (trulli houses, 1.5h), Polignano a Mare (cliffs, 30 min)

### Regional Connections Within Puglia

Once in Puglia, regional trains connect key towns:

| Route | Duration | Frequency | Price |
|-------|----------|-----------|-------|
| Bari → Brindisi | 1-1.5h | Frequent | €8-€15 |
| Bari → Lecce | 1.5h | Hourly | €12-€25 |
| Brindisi → Lecce | 30 min | Frequent | €5-€10 |

---

## General Train Travel Tips

### Booking Strategy

**When to Book:**
- High-speed trains: 2-4 weeks ahead for Super Economy fares
- Regional trains: Can buy same day (no reservations)

**Where to Book:**
- Trenitalia.com or Italotreno.it (direct booking best)
- Omio, Rail Europe (third-party, often higher fees)
- Station ticket windows (if you prefer in-person)

### At the Station

**Arrival Time:**
- High-speed trains: Arrive 15-20 minutes early
- Regional trains: Arrive 10 minutes early
- Check platform (binario) on departure boards

**Validating Tickets:**
- E-tickets (QR code): No validation needed, show on phone
- Paper tickets: MUST validate in yellow machines before boarding
- Failure to validate = fine from conductor

### On the Train

**Luggage:**
- Store large bags in designated racks (near doors or overhead)
- Keep valuables with you
- Watch bags during stops

**Seats:**
- High-speed: Assigned seats (on ticket)
- Regional: Open seating (first-come, first-served)

**Quiet Cars:**
- Some trains have "Silenzio" cars (no phone calls, quiet conversations)

### Train Classes Compared

**Trenitalia Classes:**
- **Standard/2nd Class:** Comfortable, affordable
- **Premium:** More space, quieter
- **Business:** Leather seats, welcome drink, snacks
- **Executive:** Most spacious, full meal service (rare on our routes)

**Italo Classes:**
- **Smart:** Economy
- **Comfort:** Business equivalent
- **Prima:** First class equivalent

---

## Station Information

### Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN)

**Address:** Piazza della Stazione, 50123 Firenze
**Key Features:**
- Central location (walk to Duomo in 10 min)
- Luggage storage (€6-€12 per bag per day)
- Pharmacy, cafes, shops
- Free WiFi

**Getting to Hotels:**
- Many Florence hotels walkable
- Taxi stand outside main entrance
- Trams and buses available

### Napoli Centrale

**Address:** Piazza Garibaldi, 80142 Napoli
**Key Features:**
- Main Naples station (also called "Garibaldi" due to piazza location)
- Can be crowded - watch belongings
- Metro connections (Line 1 and 2)
- Luggage storage available
- Taxi stand (use official white taxis only)

**Safety Note:**
- Busy station attracts pickpockets
- Keep bags close and zipped
- Use official taxis (white with meters)

### Bari Centrale

**Address:** Piazza Aldo Moro, 70122 Bari
**Key Features:**
- Modern, clean station
- Luggage storage
- 15-20 min walk to Bari Vecchia (old town)
- Good cafes and restaurants nearby

### Brindisi Stazione

**Address:** Piazza Vittorio Emanuele III, 72100 Brindisi
**Key Features:**
- Smaller station
- 10 min walk to historic center
- Taxi stand available
- Ferry terminal to Greece nearby (we won't use)

---

## Luggage Storage

**Available at:**
- Firenze SMN: Yes (€6-€12/bag/day)
- Napoli Centrale: Yes
- Bari Centrale: Yes
- Brindisi: Limited

**Useful For:**
- Day trips from base city
- Early hotel checkout but late train
- Exploring city upon arrival before hotel check-in

---

## Important Links

### Booking
- [Trenitalia](https://www.trenitalia.com)
- [Italo](https://www.italotreno.it)
- [Omio](https://www.omio.com) (multi-modal search)

### Station Info
- [Firenze SMN](https://www.trenitalia.com/en/customer_information/stazioni/firenze_santa_maria_novella.html)
- [Napoli Centrale](https://www.trenitalia.com/en/customer_information/stazioni/napoli_centrale.html)

### Apps
- **Trenitalia App** (iOS/Android) - Book, manage tickets, live updates
- **Italo Treno App** - Same for Italo trains
- **Google Maps** - Good for finding platforms and connections

---

## Train Travel Checklist

### Before Booking
- [ ] Check departure/arrival stations
- [ ] Compare Trenitalia vs. Italo pricing
- [ ] Decide on fare class
- [ ] Note cancellation/change policies

### After Booking
- [ ] Save tickets to phone (screenshot or download PDF)
- [ ] Add to calendar with station names and times
- [ ] Note platform if known
- [ ] Check for any schedule changes closer to date

### Day Before Travel
- [ ] Verify train status online
- [ ] Check weather (impacts luggage choices)
- [ ] Pack essentials in carry-on (water, snacks, book)
- [ ] Charge phone/devices

### Day of Travel
- [ ] Arrive station 15-20 min early
- [ ] Check departure board for platform (binario)
- [ ] Have ticket ready (QR code or validated paper)
- [ ] Confirm car/seat number on train
- [ ] Enjoy the ride!

---

**Next Steps:** See [Accommodations](accommodations.qmd) for where we're staying in each city.
